data.narrative:
Castelion, a Series B defense and security technology company founded by Bryon Hargis, Sean Pitt, and Andrew Kreitz, is evaluated against a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms. The private comp set includes same-stage peers such as Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Defense Unicorns, and Heven AeroTech, which anchor the valuation framework.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the company's last post-money valuation of $1.0B, implying $1.9B enterprise value. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B. These two approaches—each directly applicable given Castelion's stage and sector positioning—converge on an overlapping midpoint.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.2B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median estimate of $1.6B. This range reflects the current $1.0B+ post-money valuation and accounts for normal Series B step-up dynamics. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of the next financing event, relative competitive positioning versus the identified peer set, and revenue traction metrics not captured in this dataset.
